title: In re Tesla Autopilot Litigation — consolidated product liability class action
summary: Consolidated class action alleging Tesla's Autopilot and Full Self-Driving systems are defective and that Tesla misrepresented their capabilities to consumers. Plaintiffs allege injuries arising from collisions and near-misses attributable to system failures.

title: Beijing Consumers Association v. Zhejiang Geely Holding Group — battery fire disclosure claim

parties.plaintiff: Beijing Consumers Association (北京市消费者协会)
parties.defendant: Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd.

summary: Consumer advocacy organization alleges Geely failed to timely disclose known battery thermal runaway defects in the Galaxy E8 model, resulting in underreporting of fire incidents to SAMR. Claims violation of the Consumer Rights Protection Law (消费者权益保护法) Art. 20 and the Product Quality Law Art. 27.

title: Ford Motor Co. v. Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. (CATL) — trade secret misappropriation re LFP cell manufacturing
summary: Ford alleges CATL misappropriated proprietary manufacturing processes for lithium iron phosphate (LFP) cells developed during their joint BlueOval SK battery venture. Parallel proceedings filed in both E.D. Michigan and Shenzhen IP Court. Case raises novel questions of cross-border trade secret enforcement and choice of law.

title: Li Mou v. BYD Auto Co., Ltd. — lemon law claim, range degradation after OTA software update
summary: Plaintiff alleged that BYD's OTA update (v2.3.1, released Sep 2025) reduced the Han EV's rated range by 18% without consumer consent or adequate disclosure, constituting a product defect under the Three-Package Rules for Automobiles. Court awarded full refund plus 10% punitive supplement under the Consumer Rights Protection Law Art. 55.

title: State of California v. Volkswagen Group of America, Inc. — residual emissions fraud enforcement
summary: California AG alleges Volkswagen continued to deploy defeat device logic in MY2022–2024 turbocharged ICE vehicles sold in California, in violation of the state's 2020 consent decree arising from the Dieselgate settlement. Action seeks civil penalties of $37,500 per violation under the California Clean Air Act and Health & Safety Code § 43016.

title: Wang Xiaoming v. NIO, Inc. — sudden unintended acceleration resulting in highway collision
summary: Plaintiff alleges NIO's ES8 accelerated uncontrollably at highway speeds due to a software defect in the drive control unit, causing a rear-end collision with personal injury. NIO disputes the characterization and points to driver error data from on-board telemetry. Case involves competing expert testimony on EDR data interpretation.

title: Rivian Automotive, Inc. v. Scout Motors Inc. — trade dress infringement, vehicle design
summary: Rivian alleges Scout's Terra pickup truck and Traveler SUV designs improperly copy Rivian's distinctive 'stadium lighting' front lamp design and overall vehicle silhouette trade dress, creating consumer confusion in the electric truck market. Motion to dismiss pending; Scout argues trade dress is functional and lacks secondary meaning.

title: Chen v. SAIC-GM-Wuling Automobile — airbag non-deployment, Hongguang MINI EV
summary: Plaintiff sustained serious injuries in a frontal collision when airbags failed to deploy in the Hongguang MINI EV at speeds triggering deployment per specifications. Court found product defect under the Product Quality Law Art. 41, ordering compensation of ¥340,000. Defendant has appealed to the Guangxi Higher People's Court.
